fixture_note: FIXTURE — synthetic shape modeled on the paysponge/coingecko onchain DEX API x402 responses. NO real market data. Used by N3 sim AND as the placeholder for the N4 paysh_live recorded fixture (overwritten when operator runs pytest -m live_paysh). Min price USD is 0 (free tier) — the live caller's budget cap still applies but this provider may not actually settle a USDC charge.

headers.X-Payment-Required: x402; network=solana-mainnet; asset=usdc; amount=0.001; recipient=<provider_pubkey>; nonce=<server_nonce>

body.narrative: CoinGecko Onchain DEX returned the top trending pools across the requested network slice. For a Solana-native neobank the relevant frame is the USDC-paired top-10 by 24h volume on Raydium and Orca; that pool list anchors the market-making surface a neobank's swap product would sit on top of. Pool metadata returned per entry: token address, paired-token address, fee tier, 24h volume USD, 24h price change, 1h price change, market cap, FDV, and liquidity USD. The trending heuristic CoinGecko uses is a hybrid of volume-velocity and address-uniqueness; this catches new launches faster than pure-volume rankings but introduces noise on tokens with airdrop-driven address counts. For the neobank use-case the recommended filter post-fetch is: liquidity_usd > $250k AND fee_tier in {0.01%, 0.05%}, which removes the long tail of memecoin pools that aren't routable for neobank-grade swap quotes. Latency: 380ms p50, 920ms p99 against the Solana slice. The free-tier cap on this endpoint applies per-IP rather than per-wallet so high-frequency callers should bind to a residential proxy pool or upgrade. For neobanks doing on-chain settlement of card authorizations, this endpoint is sufficient to run the FX-routing decision without a paid CoinGecko Pro key.
